LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER AURORA ADVISORS INCORPORATED'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE UNDER THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT IS DENIED FOR REASONS STATED ON THE RECORD. THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IS ALSO DENIED. THE COURT OVERRULED THE OBJECTIONS TO THE GREGORY DECLARATIONS AND SUSTAINED THE FOLLOWING OBJECTIONS TO MICHAEL OLENICKS' 2ND DECLARATION: 7, 10, 11, 12, 14 AND 15. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate Under The California Public Records Act
Off Calendar May-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Timothy Y. Fong, Esq. of the Law Office of Timothy Fong, Tel. 408.627.7810, for petitioner and moving party;
Edward Gregory, Esq., Tel. 213.439.9433, and Jennifer Morrow, Esq., Tel. 213.439.9426, of Steptoe & Johnson LLP, for respondent and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. Mr. Christopher Philips, who is not a party to the case, was sworn and examined. At the conclusion of the hearing, the Court ruled as follows: PETITIONER AURORA ADVISORS INCORPORATED's Petition For Writ Of Mandate Under The California Public Records Act is denied for reasons stated on the record. The request for attorney's fees and costs is also denied. The Court overruled the objections to the Gregory Declarations and sustained the following objections to Michael Olenicks' 2nd Declaration: 7, 10, 11, 12, 14 and 15. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
